Model Defaults and Simulation Helpers

Source: R/model_utils.R
model_setup.Rd

These functions expose the default parameterisation, initial conditions, raw Hermosillo study data loader, and the ODE simulation workflow used throughout the package.

Usage

default_parameters()

default_state(parameters = default_parameters())

load_study_data(
  path = system.file("extdata", "raw", "study_data_dengue_hemorrhagic_fever.csv", package
    = "twostraindengue"),
  rows = 35:50
)

run_model(
  initial_state,
  time_grid,
  parameters,
  ode_function = dengue_model_ode
)

extract_reported_cases(model_output)

Arguments

parameters

Named numeric parameter vector.

path

Path to the raw study CSV. By default, this resolves to the file shipped under inst/extdata/raw/.

rows

Integer row indices to select from the raw study data.

initial_state

Named numeric state vector passed to deSolve::ode().

time_grid

Numeric vector of solver times.

ode_function

ODE callback used by deSolve::ode(). Defaults to the package's internal two-strain dengue system.

model_output

Matrix or data frame returned by run_model().

Value

A named numeric vector containing the default model parameters.

A named numeric vector containing the default initial state for the ODE system, including the cumulative reported-case state z.

A data frame with columns week, classical, and hemorrhagic.

A matrix of ODE output as returned by deSolve::ode().

A two-column matrix with classical and hemorrhagic reported-case trajectories extracted from the ODE output.

Examples

parameters <- default_parameters()
state <- default_state(parameters)
time_grid <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)

model_output <- run_model(state, time_grid, parameters)
head(extract_reported_cases(model_output))
#>      classical hemorrhagic
#> [1,]  4.000000   1.0000000
#> [2,]  4.574777   0.9295072
#> [3,]  5.154836   0.8702416
#> [4,]  5.747903   0.8217707
#> [5,]  6.360186   0.7837030
#> [6,]  6.996752   0.7556928
